name: Alexi Giannoulias
bio: Alexi Giannoulias has served as Illinois Secretary of State since January 2023, elected in November 2022. He previously served as Illinois State Treasurer from 2007 to 2011. In 2010, he ran for the U.S. Senate seat vacated by Barack Obama, losing to Republican Mark Kirk by 2 percentage points. His family owned Broadway Bank in Chicago, which failed in April 2010 during the financial crisis — an issue that dogged his 2010 Senate campaign. As Secretary of State, he has focused on driver's license access, voting rights, and digitizing state services.
prior_office: Illinois Secretary of State (2023–present); Illinois State Treasurer (2007–2011)

jacket.note: Running uncontested in Democratic primary. Finance data for Secretary of State race pending ILSBE lookup.

red_flags.title: Broadway Bank failure — family bank collapsed during 2010 financial crisis
red_flags.description: Broadway Bank, owned by the Giannoulias family, was seized by federal regulators and failed in April 2010. The bank had made loans to convicted criminals including Tony Rezko and Michael 'Jaws' Giorango, and had expanded its risky loan portfolio significantly under Alexi's tenure as senior loan officer (2002-2006) before he became State Treasurer. The failure became a major issue in his 2010 U.S. Senate race.

news_hits.title: 2026 Illinois Primary Live Results
news_hits.summary: 270toWin election day results tracker confirms Alexi Giannoulias is unopposed in the Democratic primary for his second term as Illinois Secretary of State.

news_hits.title: Illinois Secretary Of State Race: Primary Election 2026
news_hits.summary: Two Republican candidates competing in the March 17 primary for the chance to face Democrat incumbent Alexi Giannoulias in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Your guide to the March 2026 primary in DuPage County
news_hits.summary: Alexi Giannoulias, incumbent Democrat, has filed to run for reelection as Illinois Secretary of State. Walter Adamczyk and Diane M. Harris filed as Republican challengers.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias runs unopposed in Democratic Secretary of State primary
news_hits.summary: Incumbent Democratic Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias is running unopposed in his primary for reelection, automatically advancing to the November general election. For the GOP nomination for secretary of state, Joliet resident Diane M. Harris is competing against Chicagoan Walter Adamczyk. The winner will face Giannoulias in November; Giannoulias is a heavy favorite in the blue state.

news_hits.title: Juliana Stratton, Daniel Biss, Darren Bailey among winners in 2026 Illinois primary elections
news_hits.summary: Sun-Times election night recap confirms Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic Secretary of State primary, advancing automatically to the November general election. Giannoulias was first elected in 2022 and is seeking his second term.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC's Power Is Tested
news_hits.summary: Post-primary coverage of statewide races confirms Giannoulias advances unopposed as Democratic Secretary of State nominee for November 2026.

news_hits.title: Who won the 2026 Illinois primary election? Latest results, and which races remain too close to call
news_hits.summary: NBC Chicago primary results roundup confirms incumbent Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic primary. He will face Republican Diane Harris (who secured the GOP nomination) in the November general election. With no primary challenge, Giannoulias is positioned as a strong favorite in the heavily Democratic state.

news_hits.title: Alexi Giannoulias runs unopposed for second term as Illinois Secretary of State
news_hits.summary: Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias ran unopposed in the Democratic primary, advancing automatically to the November general election where he will seek his second four-year term. He will face Republican nominee Diane Harris of Joliet, who defeated Walter Adamczyk in the GOP primary.

news_hits.title: Two low-funded Republicans battle to run against Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias in the fall
news_hits.summary: Tribune preview of the Republican primary to oppose Giannoulias in November. Notes two underfunded Republicans contested the GOP primary while Giannoulias ran unopposed on the Democratic side. In the 2022 general election Giannoulias won with 54% over Republican Dan Brady. He is heavily favored for reelection in the November general election.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias Cruises: Unopposed in Democratic Primary, GOP Brawls for Illinois Secretary of State
news_hits.summary: Alexi Giannoulias advances unopposed in the Democratic primary. On the Republican side, Diane Harris and Walter Adamczyk contested the GOP nomination. Giannoulias heads to the general as a heavy favorite.

news_hits.title: Illinois Secretary of State Primary Election 2026 Live Results
news_hits.summary: NBC News confirms Giannoulias is unopposed in the Democratic primary as he seeks a second term as Illinois Secretary of State.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias cash surge fuels City Hall buzz
news_hits.summary: Politico Illinois Playbook reports Alexi Giannoulias is accumulating substantial campaign cash ahead of his unopposed Secretary of State primary, with growing speculation he may be positioning for a 2027 Chicago mayoral run. The cash buildup is unusual for an uncontested incumbent, fueling political chatter about his broader ambitions.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias banks $9M amid mayoral buzz
news_hits.summary: Politico Illinois Playbook reports a scoop: Illinois Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias has logged a stunning $9 million in campaign contributions — an extraordinary amount for an uncontested statewide incumbent — fueling growing talk that he is laying the groundwork to run for mayor of Chicago in 2027. Giannoulias faces no Democratic primary opponent and is a heavy November general election favorite against Republican Diane Harris.

news_hits.title: Giannoulias Joins Illinois Literacy Foundation, YMCA to Celebrate National Read Across America Day
news_hits.summary: Secretary of State Giannoulias joined the Illinois Literacy Foundation and YMCA of Metropolitan Chicago for a Day of Service on March 2, 2026 — National Read Across America Day — donating books and participating in literacy programming. Official press release from the SOS office.
